overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains an active presence on Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, with a total follower count of 3580. The content focuses on research related to disinformation, coordinated inauthentic behavior, computational social science, and related job/funding opportunities. Engagement varies across platforms, with LinkedIn posts generally receiving more likes and comments compared to Mastodon and Bluesky.

contentThemes.description: Posts about research findings, publications, and presentations related to computational social science.
Content focusing on the study and detection of disinformation campaigns and coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media.
Announcements of research grants, postdoctoral positions, and PhD student opportunities.

recommendations: Tailor content more specifically to each platform's audience to maximize engagement.
Experiment with different content formats (e.g., videos, infographics) to see what resonates best with each platform's audience.
Increase the frequency of posts on platforms like Mastodon and Bluesky to maintain visibility and engagement.
Actively engage with comments and replies on all platforms to foster a sense of community and encourage further interaction.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky posts are performing well in terms of reposts, indicating the content is shareable and resonating with the community. Focus on topics related to academic research and conferences to further boost visibility.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon engagement is lower compared to other platforms. Focus on making posts more easily discoverable and explore participating in relevant hashtags/communities.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn generates the highest engagement, especially with professional announcements such as grants and job opportunities. Continuing to share similar content is likely to perform well.

topPerformingContent.content: Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.

Now hiring: Two postdoctoral researchers to join our team working on computational methods for social media analysis. Background in data science, computational social science, or related fields preferred.
Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
